Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Dec-18-2024 Continued To Jan-30-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing re:PLAINTIFF JIA LIU's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents.

No appearances made. The court adopts the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to compel is continued to January 30 to enable supplemental briefing. At the crux of this lemon law discovery dispute is a question neither side grapples with from a legal perspective: when a party is involved in many similar actions simultaneously, is it obliged to prepare a sufficient number of persons most knowledgeable to enable timely PMK depositions? Plaintiff served its first PMK notice in May 2024, but the earliest deposition date defendant has proffered was 11 months out, in April 2025. This defendant says - without legal citation - is because it does not have enough PMKs. (Opp. 6:10-18, 26; 9:15-18; 19:9-12.) But plaintiff says - also without legal citation - defendant "is acutely aware of the volume of cases filed each year yet has chosen not to hire/train/accommodate additional PMKs." (Rply. 3:11-13.) Each side shall file and serve a legal brief (maximum five pages) on this issue by January 15.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr; Clerk:K. Valdes; Not Reported.(302/RBU)
